data.description:
## Why

The two new constructs (overlay `when`/`enabled`, the `batch` step) need an end-to-end proof that
they compose and run, independent of the plan-review migration. A small disposable workflow is the
cheapest proof and doubles as the visual-editing refinement sample.

## What

Author a throwaway demo workflow under examples/ that exercises BOTH new constructs: a couple of
conditionally-included (overlay) prompt steps + a `batch` step over a small prompt group, runnable
end-to-end under the FakeAgentRunner (offline, no live calls). Add a dry-run test that executes it
and asserts the constructs behave (resolved overlay set journaled; batch-plan journaled; no live
calls). Label it clearly DISPOSABLE — epic B removes it after the migration, retaining it meanwhile
as the visual-editing refinement sample.

## Scope

- src/rebar/llm/workflow/examples/<demo>.yaml (clearly labeled throwaway).
- A test (tests/unit/workflow/) running it under FakeAgentRunner and asserting overlay resolution +
  batch journaling, with zero live calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A demo workflow under examples/ uses BOTH the overlay predicate and the `batch` step.
- [ ] A test runs it end-to-end under FakeAgentRunner (offline) and asserts the resolved overlay set
      is journaled and the batch-plan is journaled; no live LLM calls occur.
- [ ] The workflow is clearly labeled disposable (removed by epic B; meanwhile the visual-editing
      sample).
- [ ] make lint + the workflow test suite pass.
data.title: Throwaway demo workflow exercising overlay + batch end-to-end (offline dry-run)
